---
title: "Getting started with brfssTools"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting started with brfssTools}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>",
  eval     = FALSE
)
```

This vignette walks through brfssTools end-to-end: from a fresh install
to a tidy crosswalk you can edit and version-control alongside your
analysis. Code chunks are not executed at vignette build time — run
them yourself against your real data.

## What this package solves

The Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) collects health
data the same way every year, but the *files* don't reflect that
sameness. Column names change. Response codes shift. Variables get
renamed (`SEX1` → `SEXVAR`), calculated variables get rewritten
(`INCOME2` becomes `INCOME3` with a different bracket structure), and
multi-year analyses end up requiring a per-year dictionary you maintain
by hand.

brfssTools is that dictionary, but as a structured tool: an editable
crosswalk that maps each conceptual variable to its actual column name
in each year's data file, with support for cells that need to be
*calculated* from other columns.

The crosswalk is **anchored on your most recent year of data**: the
package reads your latest year's variable list, treats each variable
as a "concept," and builds a draft crosswalk for you to fill in
backwards through the editor.

## Common workflows

Three concrete recipes covering the most common scenarios. Pick the
one that fits, then read the rest of the vignette for what each step
is doing.

### State-data analyst (typical case)

You have a directory of `.csv` or `.xpt` files for your state, plus
(optionally) codebook documentation.

```r
library(brfssTools)

# Once per session: register the data
brfss_set_pool("OR", data_path = "Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS")

# First time only: draft the crosswalk
brfss_draft_crosswalk(dataset = "OR")

# Each working session: open the editor
brfss_crosswalk_editor(dataset = "OR")
```

### National BRFSS analyst

You want CDC's public National data and don't have your own files.

```r
library(brfssTools)

# Downloads CDC LLCP files to a cache, registers as the "National" pool
brfss_use_national(years = 2018:2024)

# Draft + edit
brfss_draft_crosswalk(dataset = "National")
brfss_crosswalk_editor(dataset = "National")
```

(A pre-built National crosswalk is planned for a future release; once
shipped, the draft step won't be needed for National-only users.)

### Migrating from v0.1.0 or pre-release v0.2.0

You have an existing crosswalk file from v0.1.0:

```r
library(brfssTools)

# Migrate the file in place; original is backed up to <path>.v01.bak
brfss_migrate_crosswalk_to_v2(path = "path/to/old/crosswalk.csv")

# Register a pool that points at the migrated file
brfss_set_pool(
  "OR",
  data_path = "Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS",
  crosswalk = "path/to/old/crosswalk.csv"   # the now-migrated v0.2.0 file
)

brfss_crosswalk_editor(dataset = "OR")
```

If you used a pre-release v0.2.0 build with a config dir at
`tools::R_user_dir("brfssTools", "config")`:

```r
brfss_migrate_config_dir(
  target_dir = "C:/projects/oregon/documentation",
  dataset    = "OR"
)
# Then register a pool pointing at the migrated location
brfss_set_pool(
  "OR",
  data_path     = "Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS",
  codebook_path = "C:/projects/oregon/documentation",
  crosswalk     = "C:/projects/oregon/documentation/OR_crosswalk.csv"
)
```

## How files are organized

A brfssTools project has three pieces, which can live anywhere on your
filesystem:

- **Data**: the raw BRFSS files (`.csv` or `.xpt`), one per year.
  Often on a shared/secure drive.
- **Codebooks** (optional): one CSV per year with question text and
  value labels. Lets the editor show context as you map cells.
- **Crosswalk**: the file you build via this package, mapping concepts
  to variables across years.

When you register a pool, by default the codebooks live in
`<data_path>/documentation/` and the crosswalk lives in
`<codebook_path>/<dataset>_crosswalk.csv`. Most users only need to
specify the data directory; the rest gets sensible defaults.

Power users with a read-only data drive can put the codebooks and
crosswalk in a project-local folder by passing `codebook_path` and/or
`crosswalk` explicitly.

## 1. Install

```r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("nfparsons/brfssTools")
```

## 2. Register your pool

```r
library(brfssTools)

# Typical case: just point at the data directory
brfss_set_pool("OR", data_path = "Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS")
#> Scanning data_path for dataset 'OR'...
#> Indexed 13 file(s) for dataset 'OR'.
#>   data_path:     Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS
#>   codebook_path: Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/documentation
#>   crosswalk:     Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/documentation/OR_crosswalk.csv
#>     (does not yet exist; run brfss_draft_crosswalk())
```

If your data is on a read-only share and you want to keep your
crosswalk locally:

```r
brfss_set_pool(
  dataset       = "OR",
  data_path     = "Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS",
  codebook_path = "C:/projects/oregon/documentation",
  crosswalk     = "C:/projects/oregon/OR_crosswalk.csv"
)
```

Pool registration is session-scoped — re-run on each R session, most
easily via your project's setup script or `.Rprofile`.

Confirm the registration:

```r
brfss_status()
#>
#> brfssTools status
#> -----------------
#>
#> Dataset: OR
#>   data_path:     Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS
#>   codebook_path: Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/documentation  (0 codebook file(s))
#>   crosswalk:     Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/documentation/OR_crosswalk.csv  (does not yet exist; run brfss_draft_crosswalk())
#>   data files:    13 file(s), years 2012-2024
```

## 3. (Optional) Set up codebooks for richer editor context

The editor displays question text and value labels for each cell when
properly-formatted codebook CSVs are present. This is **optional** —
the editor falls back to using variable names from your data files
without context, but it's noticeably easier to work with codebooks in
place.

Codebooks go in the registered codebook directory, one CSV per year:

```
Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/
├── 2018.csv
├── 2024.csv
├── ...
└── documentation/
    ├── OR_2018_codebook.csv
    └── OR_2024_codebook.csv
```

Format spec and AI-agent prompt for converting raw codebooks (PDF,
Word, etc.) are shipped with the package:

```r
# Show the format spec
brfss_codebook_help("spec")

# Show the AI-agent prompt (paste into Claude/ChatGPT alongside your raw codebook)
brfss_codebook_help("prompt")
```

Validate a codebook after dropping it in:

```r
brfss_load_codebook(dataset = "OR", year = 2024)
```

If validation passes, the editor will show codebook context for cells
in that (dataset, year). Skip ahead if you'd rather get straight to
mapping.

## 4. Draft the crosswalk

```r
brfss_draft_crosswalk(dataset = "OR")
#> Drafting from OR's most recent year (2024): Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/2024.csv
#> Found 482 variables in 2024.
#> Auto-assigned domains: 312 assigned, 170 unassigned (need user review).
#> Wrote 6266 rows to Z:/Secure_Data/Oregon_BRFSS/documentation/OR_crosswalk.csv
#> Next: open the editor and assign domains to 170 Unassigned concepts.
```

What just happened:

1. Read the most recent year's variable list (2024 in this example)
2. Sanitized each variable name into an R-friendly `concept_id`
3. Created one row per concept × year (state_var = NA in older years,
   awaiting your mapping)
4. Auto-assigned a `domain` and `subdomain` where the concept name
   matches CDC's known variables; left others as "Unassigned"
5. Wrote the result to the registered crosswalk path

The crosswalk is now a real file you can git-track, share, or edit by
hand if you want.

## 5. Edit the crosswalk

```r
brfss_crosswalk_editor(dataset = "OR")
```

The editor has three panes:

- **Left**: domains, with concept counts. "Unassigned" is pinned at
  the bottom — concepts whose domain couldn't be auto-assigned.
- **Center**: a heatmap for the selected domain. Rows are concepts;
  columns are years; cells show mapping status.
- **Right**: edit panel for the selected cell.

### Cell colors

- 🟢 mapped + verified
- 🟡 mapped, unverified
- ⚪ unmapped
- 🔵 calculated
- ⚫ unassigned domain (not editable until you assign)

### Per-cell mapping

Click a cell. The right panel shows a searchable dropdown of that
year's variables (filtered to exclude variables already used by other
concepts in the same year). Pick one, click **Save**.

```
Concept    PHYSHLTH      [Rename] [Merge…] [Delete]
Year       2018
Domain     Behavioral Health · Healthy Days
─────────
[ ] Calculated

state_var (from 2018):
[ PHYSHLTH                            ▼ ]   ← type to search

[Save] [Verify] [Clear]
```

If a codebook entry exists for the selected variable, the right panel
also shows the question text and value labels.

### Per-cell calculation

When a concept is derived from multiple columns (e.g., race calculated
from `_MRACE1` and `_HISPANC`), check the **Calculated** box. The
dropdown is replaced with a YAML editor:

```yaml
type: categorical_map

inputs:
  mrace: _MRACE1
  hisp: _HISPANC

levels:
  - value: 1
    label: "White only, NH"
    when: mrace == 1 & hisp == 2
  - value: 2
    label: "Black only, NH"
    when: mrace == 2 & hisp == 2
  - value: 8
    label: "Hispanic"
    when: hisp == 1
  - value: 9
    label: "Refused/Don't know"
    when: TRUE
```

Calculations are stored **per-cell**. The same concept can be a column
lookup in 2024 and a calculated cell in 2018 — useful for handling
year-by-year structural changes.

### Concept-level actions

The right panel header has three buttons next to the concept name:

- **Rename**: change the concept name across all years.
- **Merge…**: move this concept's mappings into another, then delete
  this one. Errors if both have non-empty mappings for the same year.
- **Delete**: remove the concept and all its rows.

### Unassigned concepts

The Unassigned section shows concepts as a list (not a heatmap), each
with an "Assign domain..." button. Until you assign a domain, the
concept's cells aren't editable.

### Saving

Edits are kept in memory until you click **Save** in the header. That
writes to disk with a `.bak` rotation. **Reload** discards in-memory
changes (with confirmation if dirty).

## 6. Adding new years

When a new year of data arrives, drop the file into your data
directory, re-register the pool (so the file is indexed), then:

```r
brfss_redraft_crosswalk(dataset = "OR")
```

This is idempotent: existing concepts and mappings survive, but new
rows are added for any new years, and new concepts are created for
variables that don't already exist.

## 7. National BRFSS data

For users working with CDC's public National BRFSS data:

```r
brfss_use_national(years = 2018:2024)
# Downloads CDC LLCP files to a cache, registers as the "National" pool

brfss_draft_crosswalk(dataset = "National")
brfss_crosswalk_editor(dataset = "National")
```

A pre-built National crosswalk is planned for a future release; until
then, drafting from the most recent year and editing is the same
workflow as for state data.

## Sharing your work

Your crosswalk is a regular CSV file at the path you registered. Move
it however you'd move any file: copy to another machine, commit to git,
share via cloud storage. To use it on another machine, register a pool
that points at the same crosswalk path.

## Migration

If you have a v0.1.0 brfssTools crosswalk:

```r
brfss_migrate_crosswalk_to_v2(
  path = "path/to/old/crosswalk.csv",
  dry_run = TRUE
)

brfss_migrate_crosswalk_to_v2(path = "path/to/old/crosswalk.csv")
# Backs up to <path>.v01.bak; writes the migrated file in place.
```

If you used a pre-release v0.2.0 build with a config dir at
`tools::R_user_dir("brfssTools", "config")`:

```r
brfss_migrate_config_dir(
  target_dir = "C:/projects/oregon/documentation",
  dataset    = "OR"
)
# Copies your crosswalk to <target>/OR_crosswalk.csv. Source preserved
# until you re-run with remove_source = TRUE.
```

After migrating, you'll likely have CDC's year-versioned variable
groups as separate concepts (e.g., `ADDEPEV2` and `ADDEPEV3`). Use the
editor's **Merge** button to consolidate them.

## Troubleshooting

**`No pool registered for dataset 'OR'`** — re-run `brfss_set_pool()`.
Pool registration is session-scoped.

**Editor opens but the heatmap is empty** — the crosswalk has zero
rows. Run `brfss_draft_crosswalk(dataset = "OR")`.

**Editor shows v0.1.0 schema error** — your crosswalk predates the
v0.2.0 schema. Run `brfss_migrate_crosswalk_to_v2(path = ...)`.

**A merge fails with "Cannot merge: both concepts have mappings for
year X"** — both concepts have non-empty mappings for the same year.
Decide which is correct, clear the wrong one (Clear button in the
edit panel), then retry.

**`brfss_pull()` says it's not implemented** — correct. Pull is
deferred to a follow-up release; v0.2.0 focuses on building the
crosswalk.
